title: Fixed #30509 -- Various FileResponse fixes and changes

issues.body: I'm not sure the rename here is worth the churn.

issues.body: I'm not sure either, but this way it just seems more how it should have been from the beginning... imo. And will it ever be more worth? Because i'm pretty sure it's only the _churn_ will only get bigger and messier with time.
How about instead/additionally we remove that method from the docs, so people don't try to call it manually, unless they find it themselves to override it?
Had to add some minor magic so it's possible to get back to the initial position when `tell` isn't available.

issues.body: This is already covered by `test_file_from_disk_as_attachment()`
I'm thinking a single test setting the `custom_name` would be enough.

This should live in FileResponse.__init__() checking if content_type was not None in `kwargs`. 

The variable name should either be something like `use_default_...` or `guess_default_...` or else `explicit_..._set` or similar.

issues.body:
Are we really testing the Content-Type setting here? 

It strikes me this test is really about the Content-Disposition behaviour. We should focus it to that. (With a separate case for testing 'Content-Type' branched (such as guessing the content type from the filename.)
This **was** covered by `test_file_from_disk_as_attachment()` until i [*merged*](https://github.com/django/django/pull/11414#issuecomment-497459343) it into this one combined test :)

Is the lack of preceding underscore on purpose?
Also i don't agree with the idea of `use_default_content_type` or `guess_default_content_type` - after all, it's used to inform `set_headers()` to NOT use the default content_type value, and "guessing default"... excuse me, but what would that even me
